title: Review-to-Knowledge Worker

story.i_want_to: automatically extract knowledge articles from review findings via a background worker
story.so_that: the knowledge wiki grows organically from every code review, creating a compounding learning loop

rationale: Code reviews surface patterns, conventions, and decisions that represent valuable institutional knowledge, but this knowledge typically evaporates after the review is complete. By automatically extracting knowledge article candidates from review findings via an Oban worker, the system transforms ephemeral review feedback into durable, searchable knowledge. The worker uses compile-time DI for the LLM extractor, enabling deterministic testing with Mox while allowing the production implementation to call whatever extraction service is configured.

acceptance_criteria.description: A `Loopctl.Workers.ReviewKnowledgeWorker` Oban worker exists in the `:knowledge` queue with max_attempts: 3. It receives a review_record_id in the job args.

The worker reads the review record and its findings, then calls `Loopctl.Knowledge.ExtractorBehaviour.extract_articles/1` (resolved via compile-time DI: `@extractor Application.compile_env(:loopctl, :knowledge_extractor, Loopctl.Knowledge.LlmExtractor)`) passing the review findings.

A `Loopctl.Knowledge.ExtractorBehaviour` behaviour exists defining `@callback extract_articles(map()) :: {:ok, [map()]} | {:error, term()}`. Each returned map has keys: title (string), body (string), category (atom), tags (list of strings).

The extractor returns structured article candidates as `[%{title: string, body: string, category: atom, tags: [string]}]`. The worker creates Article records with source_type: "review_finding", source_id: review_record.id, status: :draft, and the tenant_id from the review record.

Duplicate detection: before creating an article, the worker checks if an article with the same source_type and source_id already exists for the tenant. If so, the worker skips creation and logs a warning. This makes the worker idempotent across retries.

Circuit breaker on LLM failures: if the extractor returns `{:error, term()}`, the worker returns `{:error, reason}` to let Oban handle retries up to max_attempts. After max_attempts exhausted, the job moves to the discarded state.
The worker is enqueued within the same Ecto.Multi that creates the review_record (following the EventGenerator pattern). The job args include review_record_id and tenant_id.
An audit event is created when articles are successfully extracted: event_type "knowledge.articles_extracted", payload includes review_record_id and count of articles created.

config/test.exs maps `:knowledge_extractor` to `Loopctl.MockExtractor`. The mock is defined in test/support/mocks.ex via `Mox.defmock(Loopctl.MockExtractor, for: Loopctl.Knowledge.ExtractorBehaviour)`.
The `:knowledge` Oban queue is configured in config/config.exs with appropriate concurrency (default: 5).

The worker implements a custom backoff: @impl Oban.Worker def backoff(%Oban.Job{attempt: attempt}), do: trunc(:math.pow(attempt, 4) + 15 + :rand.uniform(30) * attempt). This provides exponential backoff with jitter matching the project convention.
The worker uses Oban unique option: unique: [period: 300, keys: [:review_record_id]] to prevent duplicate job enqueue for the same review record within a 5-minute window.

test_cases.name: Worker extracts articles from review findings successfully
preconditions: MockExtractor stubbed to return {:ok, [%{title: "Pattern: GenServer Pooling", body: "...", category: :pattern, tags: ["otp", "genserver"]}]}
expected_results: Article has source_type: "review_finding" and source_id: review_record.id
Article title, body, category, tags match extractor output

test_cases.name: Worker skips duplicate extraction
preconditions: MockExtractor stubbed to return {:ok, [%{title: "...", body: "...", category: :finding, tags: []}]}

test_cases.name: Worker handles extractor failure gracefully
expected_results: Oban will retry up to max_attempts: 3

test_cases.name: Worker creates multiple articles from single review
preconditions: MockExtractor stubbed to return {:ok, [article_1, article_2, article_3]}
expected_results: Three articles created, all with status: :draft
All articles share the same source_type and source_id
Each article has distinct title and content

test_cases.name: Tenant isolation: worker only creates articles for correct tenant
preconditions: MockExtractor stubbed to return {:ok, [%{title: "Pattern", body: "...", category: :pattern, tags: []}]}

test_cases.name: Worker returns :ok when extractor returns empty list

technical_notes: Worker MUST use compile-time DI: `@extractor Application.compile_env(:loopctl, :knowledge_extractor, Loopctl.Knowledge.LlmExtractor)`. NEVER use opts-based DI.
Follow the EventGenerator pattern for enqueuing: the Oban.insert is part of the same Ecto.Multi that creates the review_record, ensuring atomicity.
The `:knowledge` Oban queue should be added to config/config.exs alongside existing queues like `:webhooks` and `:embeddings`.
Duplicate detection query: `Repo.exists?(from a in Article, where: a.source_type == ^source_type and a.source_id == ^source_id and a.tenant_id == ^tenant_id)`.
Testing: async: true, Mox.set_mox_from_context in setup, setup :verify_on_exit!, MockExtractor in test/support/mocks.ex, fixture(:review_record) in test/support/fixtures.ex.
The ExtractorBehaviour is intentionally thin -- it receives review data and returns article candidates. The production LlmExtractor implementation is a separate concern (not part of this story).

Use build(:article_candidate, attrs) in test/support/fixtures.ex for mock extractor return values. Example: build(:article_candidate, %{title: "DI Pattern", body: "Always use behaviours...", category: :pattern, tags: ["di", "testing"]})
